perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Compensation for Rights and Services. TDA agrees to pay Smith, as a consideration for the Rights and Services. 1) 25,000 Restricted common shares of TDA, a. As of the date of this agreement, Company has sold stock at $2.50/share. b. Par Value is $.0001 per share. 2) 25% royalty of net TDA net sales price. 3) Option to convert annual royalties to TDA common stock at a $10 of stock for every $1 of Royalty converted. a. TDA stock conversion price based on the previous six month average daily price b. Option is limited to 50% of TDA outstanding stock and if the option is exercised, it must be exercised when the agreement &sbsp; is in effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Compensation for Rights and Services. TDA agrees to pay Smith, as a consideration for the Rights and Services. 1) 25,000 Restricted common shares of TDA, a. As of the date of this agreement, Company has sold stock at $2.50/share. b. Par Value is $.0001 per share. 2) 25% royalty of net TDA net sales price. 3) Option to convert annual royalties to TDA common stock at a $10 of stock for every $1 of Royalty converted. a. TDA stock conversion price based on the previous six month average daily price b. Option is limited to 5% of TDA outstanding stock and if the option is exercised, it must be exercised when the agreement &sbsp; is in effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the description of 'Option' in Section 4.2. Originally, 'Option is limited to 50% of TDA outstanding stock'. The changed text now states, 'Option is limited to 5% of TDA outstanding stock.' This creates a contradiction in section 4.2 by limiting the percentage and contradicting the original agreement terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Compensation for Rights and Services. TDA agrees to pay Smith, as a consideration for the Rights and Services. 1) 25,000 Restricted common shares of TDA, a. As of the date of this agreement, Company has sold stock at $2.50/share. b. Par Value is $.0001 per share. 2) 25% royalty of net TDA net sales price. 3) Option to convert annual royalties to TDA common stock at a $10 of stock for every $1 of Royalty converted. a. TDA stock conversion price based on the previous six month average daily price b. Option is limited to 5% of TDA outstanding stock and if the option is exercised, it must be exercised when the agreement &sbsp; is in effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the 'Option is limited to 50% of TDA outstanding stock'. The changed text states 'Option is limited to 5% of TDA outstanding stock'. This is a clear, direct, and impactful conflict within the document in Section 4.2 of the agreement. Because there are conflicting obligations within a single section of the contract this makes it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. TERM 6.1 Term. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and terminate at the end of the Exclusivity Period (i.e., a three (3) year period commencing on the Effective Date). 6.2 Post-Term Sales. Upon expiration of this Agreement, TDA shall cease all uses of the Rights and/or Smith's Likeness with respect to advertising, endorsing and/or promoting TDA, but TDA shall be free to continue to distribute and sell its Golf Instruction Related Products which incorporate Smith's Likeness for up to 180 days after the expiration of the Term (although TDA may not use the Rights or Smith's Likeness to promote or advertise TDA or any of TDA's non-Golf Instruction Related Products when selling the Golf Instruction Related Products, nor can TDA highlight Smith's Likeness in its packaging or sales efforts); provided, however, that TDA shall have no such right of post-Term sales unless TDA is not in default of any of its obligations hereunder as of the date of expiration or termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERM 6.1 Term. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and terminate at the end of the Exclusivity Period (i.e., a three (3) year period commencing on the Effective Date). 6.2 Post-Term Sales. Upon expiration of this Agreement, TDA shall cease all uses of the Rights and/or Smith's Likeness with respect to advertising, endorsing and/or promoting TDA, and TDA shall not be free to continue to distribute and sell its Golf Instruction Related Products which incorporate Smith's Likeness for any period after the expiration of the Term; provided, however, that TDA shall have such right of post-Term sales even if TDA is in default of any of its obligations hereunder as of the date of expiration or termination.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text in Section 6.2 completely reverses the conditions under which TDA can engage in post-term sales. It now states TDA shall not be free to continue to distribute or sell its products after the term. Also, it provides that TDA can engage in post-term sales even if they are in default, which contradicts the original condition that 'TDA shall have no such right of post-Term sales unless TDA is not in default of any of its obligations.' This introduces a significant contradiction affecting post-term rights and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Post-Term Sales. Upon expiration of this Agreement, TDA shall cease all uses of the Rights and/or Smith's Likeness with respect to advertising, endorsing and/or promoting TDA, and TDA shall not be free to continue to distribute and sell its Golf Instruction Related Products which incorporate Smith's Likeness for any period after the expiration of the Term; provided, however, that TDA shall have such right of post-Term sales even if TDA is in default of any of its obligations hereunder as of the date of expiration or termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 6.2 regarding TDA's rights and obligations post-term. The original text conditions post-term sales on TDA not being in default, while the modified text explicitly allows post-term sales even if TDA is in default. This creates a clear conflict regarding the conditions for post-term sales, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. Also, the changed text states that TDA 'shall not be free to continue to distribute or sell its products after the term' which is in direct contradiction with the prior text.
